title: Attraction to same sex characters as an eye opener
display_title: Attraction to same sex characters as an eye opener
wikitext:
[]: Attraction to same-sex characters as an eye-opener Post by Korgan » Sat Jul 30, 2011 2:48 am Has Eva made any of you face your own sexuality after you found a character of your own gender to be... smoking hot? Cause I'd say that they ALL are (save for Keel and a few others), and damn, it's not just Kaworu that has stirred up so much sexual confusion among the male audience; me, I realized that I feel more than just sympathy and admiration towards Shinji after finishing the series. The same goes for ladies, I suppose. So confess; have you first felt "queer" for an Eva character?

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jul 30, 2011 3:14 am Oh, boy. You're going to love these threads if you've not seen them yet http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=6489 http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=9435 The author's afterword for vol11 of the manga had Sadamoto sorta-admitting that he'd gone gay for Kaworu too. For me, Rits' would make a good looking young (but not too young) thing to have as arm candy, plus brains too, but that's it.

[]: Post by Draconis » Sat Jul 30, 2011 6:02 am One word for this: Gross. Sorry bro. (Unless you meant you just really really like him and think he's awesome or something and not actually want to whip out the johnny on him.)

[]: Post by Korgan » Sat Jul 30, 2011 6:11 am Oh man, if you're afraid of being creeped out, better stay clear of ANY Japan-related forums. People are like that

[]: Post by Draconis » Sat Jul 30, 2011 6:21 am Korgan wrote: Oh man, if you're afraid of being creeped out, better stay clear of ANY Japan-related forums. People are like that

[]: Post by Reichu » Sat Jul 30, 2011 7:39 am Korgan wrote: So confess; have you first felt "queer" for an Eva character?

[]: Post by Bagheera » Sat Jul 30, 2011 9:44 am Draconis wrote: I'm not afraid of being creeped out. I'm just homophobic.

[]: Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jul 30, 2011 9:46 am But Shinji looks like he has a feminine figure in that pic. Does that count as same sex attraction?

[]: Post by Korgan » Sat Jul 30, 2011 10:17 am Uh, okay, I understand. I guess homophobic people have a right to express their views, although I'm puzzled by them. No feelings hurt

[]: Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Jul 30, 2011 10:27 am I don't know if "homophobic" is the same as "bigot" all of the time. Some people are just nervous around gays. I've professionally worked with gays twice that I know of on various film shoots. Those two people were fun and professional to work with. There may have been more, but I was simply never told. After all, why would I need to know?

[]: Post by TehDonutKing » Sat Jul 30, 2011 11:14 am Draconis wrote: I'm not afraid of being creeped out. I'm just homophobic.

[]: Post by SaltyJoe » Sat Jul 30, 2011 12:03 pm Bagheera wrote: Wow. I never expect people to admit to that sort of thing in public. But on the upside it's always nice when bigots self-identify so that we don't have to waste time wondering "are they or aren't they?"

[]: Post by Reichu » Sat Jul 30, 2011 12:23 pm SaltyJoe wrote: If i find a male character to be attractive in any media, then it is always a case of "I want to be like him." not "I want to have sex with him."

[]: Post by Xard » Sat Jul 30, 2011 12:35 pm Reichu wrote: I'm not sure I can even think of any fictional characters period with whom I would personally have sex if suddenly, magically given the opportunity...

[]: Post by Stryker » Sat Jul 30, 2011 12:40 pm Not surprising. I could find a few, but if I were able to do so, it would destroy my reputation from "just a little weird" to "batshit pedofreak". Or, at least, that's what people would call me (because people forgot what hebephilia is, and now just say pedo for anyone under the age of 16).

[]: Post by Shogo-Kun » Sat Jul 30, 2011 12:41 pm It is wrong for me to say that this image is what got me into EVA?

[]: Post by Stryker » Sat Jul 30, 2011 12:43 pm Not at all. Although, it is interesting that only a picture got you into Eva. For me, it was the AMV Hells that got me into it.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jul 30, 2011 12:45 pm Xard wrote: wow, really? I can.

[]: Post by Shogo-Kun » Sat Jul 30, 2011 12:52 pm Stryker wrote: Not at all. Although, it is interesting that only a picture got you into Eva. For me, it was the AMV Hells that got me into it.

[]: Post by Xard » Sat Jul 30, 2011 1:03 pm Mr. Tines wrote: There's a big gap between "wouldn't kick her out of bed for eating crackers" and "do want".
